issues.detail: The conflict-check PRD still contains single-tree authority language in Story 5 and Technical Considerations: self-assignment drives In Progress, PR merge directly drives Done, and out-of-band status edits are reverted without limiting that rule to the PM tree. This contradicts updated REQ-026, where PM pull/verified completion are authoritative and DEV-tree vendor-native transitions are revert-exempt.
issues.fix: Retarget those PRD passages to the two-tree rules: PM pull -> In Progress, verified requirement completion -> Done, detect-and-revert only on PM items; DEV items use vendor-native mechanics including merge-to-Done and are never NGO-facing.

issues.detail: The test requires bootstrap-bound attribution without first pulling the bootstrap PM item. Under d82 only a pulled PM requirement may bind attribution, so an implementation that binds an unpulled bootstrap item would pass.
issues.fix: Explicitly pull the bootstrap through the Skill, assert that this alone moves it to In Progress and establishes the binding, then assert authoring rows target that PM item. Also prove an attempted pre-pull binding does not resolve and is recorded unattributed.

issues.detail: No test defines the PM-tree completion outcome for the bootstrap item. A passing score pushes the DEV tree, but the suite never asserts whether or how the bootstrap reaches Done under the verified-completion-only PM rule; a failed score likewise need not leave it In Progress.
issues.fix: Specify that scorer gate-pass evidence is the bootstrap item's verified completion evidence, then assert below-threshold keeps it In Progress and a successful gate moves it to Done through the authorized completion path. If another rule is intended, state and test it explicitly.

issues.detail: The relation check proves only that each DEV item has some PM relation. Shuffled relations, every DEV item pointing to one PM requirement, or unrelated PM mutations could all pass.
issues.fix: Use at least two distinguishable PM requirements and PRD story/AC sentinels; assert every generated DEV item maps to the correct PM requirement, no DEV item is orphaned or cross-related, and the pre-gate PM tree remains unchanged except for the explicitly defined bootstrap transition.

issues.detail: The suite never exercises the new DEV-tree exemption edge. It does not prove that vendor-native DEV assignment/status/merge-to-Done survives the PM detect-and-revert machinery or that DEV state cannot drive PM status.
issues.fix: Add or cross-reference a test that takes a generated DEV item through vendor-native merge-to-Done without reversion, attempts an unauthorized equivalent PM status change and observes reversion, and confirms the DEV transition alone does not change its related PM requirement.

issues.detail: The NGO-facing assertion still checks only the bootstrap task and score state. It does not verify that the full PM tree is the sole visible authority or test the boundary after the DEV tree exists, so DEV items, statuses, comments, or relations could leak onto the project page and still pass.
issues.fix: Assert the PRD-phase page shows the scope PM requirements plus bootstrap and score state; after gate pass, assert the same PM-only projection remains and no DEV-tree item, status, comment, or relation is exposed.
